# REF https://bookdown.org/brianjmpark/everydayR/digression-i-gradient-descent-with-r.html


```r
# 500 obs for x, bet 0,1
library(tidyverse)
set.seed(123)
theta_0 <- 5
theta_1 <- 2
n_obs <- 500
x <- rnorm(n_obs)
```


```r
# y = a1*x + a2 + random
y <- theta_1*x + theta_0 + rnorm(n_obs, 0, 3)

data <- tibble(x = x, y = y)

ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y)) + 
  geom_point(size = 2) + theme_bw() + 
  labs(title = 'Simulated Data')
```


```r
# goal:  given only the data, find coef  (~ 5, ~2 ...correct, high confid)
ols <- lm(y ~ x, data = data)
summary(ols)
```

Given ols, calc usual MLE
```r
rm(theta_0, theta_1)
cost_function <- function(theta_0, theta_1, x, y){
  pred <- theta_1*x + theta_0
  res_sq <- (y - pred)^2
  res_ss <- sum(res_sq)
  return(mean(res_ss))
}

cost_function(theta_0 = ols$coefficients[1][[1]], 
         theta_1 = ols$coefficients[2][[1]],
         x = data$x, y = data$y)

```

Separately, use gradient descent to find coef 
```r
```
